scenario_config.name: before_write_buffer_ingest_isolation
_comparison_notes: traffic shape; the difference is the backend image is built from commit
6d77836 (predecessor of the Redis write-buffer introduction). At this
baseline the ingest endpoint writes synchronously to Postgres inside a
transaction.atomic block; no Redis buffer, no flusher process.
This is the headline comparison for the thesis: synchronous vs buffered
ingest at identical hardware, dataset, and traffic shape.
Expected: sustained RPS will be MUCH lower at this baseline (per the
write-buffering-adr.md, ~16–18 RPS vs the buffered version's higher
ceiling), and dropped_iterations should appear much earlier in the ramp.
Run both and diff via benchmarks/compare_reports.py.
